en: That's insane!
I mean, just because you know Max has a small penis...
Stop! Just stop!
You are kidding me.
I'm sorry, I can't do this if it means tearing down the Arcadian.
ko: 미안하지만 아카디안을 허물게 된다면 이건 할 수 없어.

en: It's an architectural landmark.
New is always better, Ted.
ko: 새로운 게 항상 더 나아, 테드.

en: That's the rule.
Just like bigger is always better.
ko: 더 크면 항상 더 좋다는 것처럼.

en: That's not true.
Sometimes smaller is better.
Hey, look how big my cell phone is.
ko: 이봐, 내 휴대폰이 얼마나 큰지 봐.

en: You'll hear people say that.
Oh, so you want something compact and efficient that fits comfortably in your pocket.
ko: 아, 그러니까 주머니에 편하게 들어가는 작고 효율적인 걸 원하는 거구나.

en: And I think Max is just a great guy.
ko: 그리고 나는 맥스가 정말 좋은 사람이라고 생각해.

en: Well, he told you. Told him what?
Max has a small penis.
How can you speak of such things?
ko: 그런 얘기를 어떻게 할 수 있어?

en: Girls talk about everything.
Size, shape, left to right leaning orientation.
Length, resemblance to a historical figure such as Winston Churchill.
ko: 길이, 윈스턴 처칠 같은 역사적 인물과의 유사성.

en: Yes, that's one of you. Girth, brooming.
ko: 맞아, 그건 너 중 하나야. 둘레, 청소.

en: How can you speak of such things?
ko: 그런 얘기를 어떻게 할 수 있어?

en: You're kidding.
Barney, every time I mention a woman you don't know, the first thing you say is boobs.
ko: 바니, 내가 모르는 여자를 언급할 때마다 네가 제일 먼저 하는 말은 가슴이야.

en: Not the complete sentence.
